title: humboltia sprucei
intro: Humboldtia sprucei, also known as the Indian poison nut tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include the presence of a toxic alkaloid called sprucein, as well as other compounds that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat infections. The herbal preparations of Humboldtia sprucei typically include extracts of the plant's bark, leaves, and seeds, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and other medicinal products.
